name: Banner repeaters
blurb: A black bar on a white disc that pre-warns you of a signal you can’t see yet.
intro: A banner repeater gives you an early read of a signal whose own sighting is poor — hidden by a curve, a bridge or a long platform. It repeats only whether that signal is “off” (a proceed aspect) or at danger. You still obey the actual signal when you reach it; the banner just lets you plan ahead.

variants.name: Banner repeater
variants.blurb: Horizontal bar = signal ahead at danger; inclined bar = signal ahead is off.

aspects.name: Horizontal bar
aspects.whatItMeans: The bar is horizontal. The signal being repeated is at danger (“on”) — be prepared to find it not off. (A single yellow counts as a proceed aspect and would instead give the inclined bar.)
aspects.whatYouDo: Control your speed so you can stop at the signal ahead, which is showing a red.
aspects.lookAlikes: A banner repeater is a black bar on a white disc, not a coloured signal. It never authorises you to pass anything — obey the real signal it repeats.
aspects.controls: Be ready to brake for the signal ahead.

aspects.name: Inclined bar
aspects.whatItMeans: The bar is inclined up to the right. The signal being repeated is “off” — it is showing a proceed aspect (green, or a single/double yellow), so it is not at danger.
aspects.whatYouDo: Continue, but still read the actual signal when it comes into view.
aspects.controls: Proceed, watching for the signal ahead.
